headline: World's First Bundibugyo Ebola Vaccine Enters Human Trials
meta_description: The world's first vaccine targeting Bundibugyo Ebola has advanced to human trials, marking a major breakthrough in fighting a rare but deadly virus.
summary_short: A historic vaccine candidate for Bundibugyo Ebola—a rare viral strain—has now entered human testing, offering hope for better outbreak response.
body:
The world's first vaccine candidate specifically designed to target Bundibugyo Ebola has advanced into human trials. This milestone represents a significant step forward in protecting against a virus that, while less frequent than other Ebola strains, poses a serious public health threat.

Bundibugyo Ebola, named after a region in Uganda where it was first identified, has sparked outbreaks over the past two decades. The development of a dedicated vaccine for this particular strain fills an important gap in global disease prevention, as previous vaccine efforts focused primarily on the more widely known Zaire strain.

This progress reflects growing recognition that rare diseases deserve targeted research and investment. As the vaccine moves through human trials, researchers will gather critical safety and effectiveness data—work that could eventually expand protective options for vulnerable populations in regions where Bundibugyo Ebola circulates.
quick_facts: First vaccine ever made specifically for Bundibugyo Ebola strain
Virus identified in Uganda, causes serious outbreaks
Now testing in human volunteers after laboratory phases
